personal.name: Divanshu Sharma
personal.title: Machine Learning Engineer, AI Engineer & Software Engineer
personal.bio: I am an ML researcher and AI engineer focused on building secure learning systems, applied LLM products, and production-grade developer tooling. My work spans privacy-preserving deep learning at Yale University, quantitative research at WorldQuant BRAIN, research-heavy speech and OCR systems, and full-stack AI products built with Python, FastAPI, React, and modern LLM tooling.

completed1.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

achievements: WorldQuant BRAIN Global Alphathon 2022 GOLD LEVEL (Top 1%) and Stage 1 Qualifier
Kaggle 2x Expert and 3,000+ algorithmic problems solved across LeetCode, CodeChef, and HackerRank
Pull Shark x3, Starstruck, YOLO, and Quickdraw GitHub achievements

experience.description: Engineered a secure, open-source deep learning library from scratch in Python and NumPy without external autograd, built around compiler-centric multi-party computation for privacy-preserving collaborative training.
achievements: Implemented a compiler-centric MPC architecture that provides verifiable cryptographic guarantees for collaborative training.
Designed a strided-convolution CNN that reached 94.62% ChestMNIST test accuracy in 5 epochs with batch size 512.
Built the system as a reusable open-source research library rather than a one-off experiment.

experience.description: Developed and backtested high-frequency alpha signals in Python and WorldQuant's simulation stack, focusing on robust signal generation, portfolio quality, and competition-grade performance.
achievements: Built and backtested 15+ alpha signals.
Achieved an average Sharpe Ratio of 1.8 in simulation.
Earned GOLD LEVEL recognition and a Top 1% ranking in the 2022 Global Alphathon.

projects.title: Groq Recruiter Portfolio
projects.description: A configuration-driven developer portfolio built with Next.js and TypeScript, featuring a Groq-powered recruiter chat experience, generated project cover art, motion-rich UI, and centralized content management.
achievements: Engineered a configuration-driven UI that centralized user data in JSON, reducing content update time by over 95%.
Integrated fluid animations and conversational navigation for recruiter workflows.
Achieved a 100/100 Lighthouse SEO score.

projects.title: End-to-End Indic ASR Optimization & Consensus Evaluation
projects.description: An advanced Hindi ASR optimization pipeline that fine-tuned Whisper-small, normalized Devanagari text, and fused multiple model outputs through a confusion-network-based consensus stage.
achievements: Reduced FLEURS benchmark WER by more than 48% with fine-tuning, label smoothing, and cosine learning rate scheduling.
Cut transcription errors across 177,000 distinct terms with Devanagari normalization and phonetic reverse transliteration.
Fused five ASR systems through word-level confusion networks and majority voting.

projects.title: RenAIssance OCR - Research-Grade Historical OCR Pipeline
projects.description: A research-grade OCR system for 16th and 17th century Spanish documents that combines CRAFT detection, SE-ResNet-BiLSTM CRNN transcription, beam-search decoding, and LLM-assisted post-processing.
achievements: Reduced character error rate from 3.44% to 0.00% on the validation set.
Scaled training to 12,792 degraded historical text samples through an augmentation-heavy 7-step preprocessing workflow.
Reached 100% word accuracy on the validation set for early modern Spanish OCR.

projects.title: Graph Data Explorer AI
projects.description: An interactive graph-based exploration system that turns 21,393 SAP order-to-cash records into a 765-node, 877-edge knowledge graph with FastAPI, SSE streaming, Gemini-backed graph querying, and a React Flow visualization layer.
achievements: Transformed 19 flat source tables into a typed knowledge graph with 765 nodes and 877 edges.
Built a two-stage LLM pipeline for natural language to graph traversal to natural language explanation.
Implemented keyword pre-filters, sandboxed execution, and forbidden-token checks as layered guardrails.

images.alt: Graph Data Explorer AI project placeholder

projects.title: ML4SCI Task Solutions Repository (CMS)
projects.description: A deep learning repository for CERN CMS and ML4SCI tasks, including photon classification, jet tagging, and particle momentum regression using CNNs and graph neural networks.
achievements: Reached 80% AUC for electron-vs-photon classification with a custom ResNet-15.
Cut training time by 30% while matching a GAT baseline with a GCN for momentum regression.
Improved quark/gluon jet classification by 7% with targeted VGG-style CNNs.

projects.title: BioMed-Retrieval
projects.description: A biomedical Retrieval-Augmented Generation system that combines PubMedBERT embeddings, Qdrant vector search, and BioMistral-7B generation for higher-quality domain-specific question answering.
achievements: Combined semantic retrieval and keyword-aware ranking for biomedical QA.
Improved response relevance by roughly 30% over baseline search.
Used domain-specific models and instruction tuning to improve answer quality.

projects.title: Processing Foundation p5.js Web Editor
projects.description: A substantial open-source contribution track to the Processing Foundation p5.js Web Editor, focused on keyboard workflow improvements, bug fixing, and responsive UI stabilization.
achievements: Introduced a keyboard shortcut that cut file creation time by about 5 seconds.
Resolved 6+ high-priority bugs in the editor workflow.
Fixed 12+ desktop and mobile responsiveness issues across the UI.

projects.title: University Admission MCP
projects.description: An end-to-end agentic admissions advisor that scrapes university requirements with Playwright, normalizes them into SQLite, serves them through an MCP server, and drives a recommendation loop via tool-calling clients.
achievements: Built independent scraper, MCP server, and agentic client layers with typed boundaries.
Exposed evaluate_chances and get_action_items as production-style MCP tools.
Added tests, strict typechecking, and cost-aware scaling guidance for large crawl volumes.

projects.title: MCP Zero to Hero
projects.description: An open-source monorepo for learning the Model Context Protocol through theory chapters and 20 progressively harder TypeScript and Python projects spanning tools, resources, prompts, integrations, and research-grade workflows.
achievements: Structured the repository into theory, shared modules, and beginner-to-research project tracks.
Built projects that teach input validation, path safety, API integration, guarded mutations, and host-mediated reasoning.
Documented exact Claude Desktop configuration snippets for every runnable MCP server.

images.alt: MCP Zero to Hero project placeholder

projects.title: LangChain: Zero to Hero — with DeepAgents
projects.description: A production-oriented curriculum for modern LangChain and LangGraph systems, spanning LCEL foundations, retrieval/state patterns, and DeepAgents-style multi-actor workflows with observability and testing.
achievements: Organized the curriculum into 20 projects across foundations, retrieval/state, and deep agent workflows.
Focused on production concerns such as structured output, observability, tracing, and resilient agent design.
Documented a clean progression from single LLM calls to cognitive multi-actor systems.

projects.title: HLD Zero to Hero
projects.description: A first-principles curriculum for high-level system design that moves from hardware and networking fundamentals through distributed systems tradeoffs into interview-ready walkthroughs and Python implementations.
achievements: Created a 12-module learning path plus 20 full design problems and Python solutions.
Packaged latency tables, BoE math, CAP/PACELC visuals, and READ interview heuristics in one repository.
Covered real distributed systems topics such as rate limiting, chat, file storage, ledgers, and service discovery.
metrics: Easy, medium, and hard problem bank

images.alt: HLD Zero to Hero project placeholder

projects.title: AI - From Scratch to Scale
projects.description: A broad AI engineering roadmap that spans math, classic ML, deep learning, transformers, RAG, tool use, multi-agent systems, and production deployment concerns such as vLLM, quantization, and AI security.
achievements: Designed a seven-phase learning roadmap from mathematical primitives to production-scale AI deployment.
Kept every lesson structured around intuition, theory, from-scratch implementation, production implementation, pitfalls, and knowledge checks.
Covered AI security and agent systems as first-class topics instead of afterthoughts.
metrics: From fundamentals to MLOps and AI security

images.alt: AI from Scratch to Scale project placeholder

projects.title: Agentic AI — Zero to Hero
projects.description: A production-style curriculum for building and hardening AI agents, covering structured extraction, memory systems, LangGraph orchestration, evaluation frameworks, prompt-injection defenses, tool sandboxes, and runtime resilience.
achievements: Built 25 modules across agent primitives, memory, orchestration, evaluation/security, and deployment/runtime.
Embedded attack scenarios, mitigations, and eval harnesses directly into each module structure.
Added verification tooling that checks every lesson for markdown completeness, syntax, and pytest collection.

images.alt: Agentic AI Zero to Hero project placeholder

projects.title: Vector Database from Scratch
projects.description: A from-scratch implementation and architectural deep dive of the HNSW approximate nearest neighbor algorithm, covering probabilistic layering, diversity heuristics, beam search invariants, and benchmark tradeoffs.
achievements: Implemented HNSW graph construction, routing, and beam search from scratch.
Documented the probabilistic and graph-theoretic invariants behind logarithmic ANN search.
Benchmarked recall and latency on 10K vectors with recall above 0.95 in the reference script.

images.alt: Vector Database from Scratch project placeholder

internship.availability: Available for immediate start on full-time, internship, and contract opportunities.
internship.workStyle: Research-minded, product-oriented, comfortable moving from first principles to production implementation.
internship.goals: I want to join a high-bar team where I can ship serious AI systems, contribute to research-heavy engineering work, and keep compounding across ML, systems, and product execution.

funFacts: Kaggle 2x Expert and active competitive programmer across LeetCode and CodeChef.
Built both research systems and educational open-source repositories spanning MCP, agent systems, and system design.
Enjoys converting difficult concepts into structured, teachable engineering artifacts.
personality.workingStyle: I like to build from first principles, validate aggressively, and keep shipping while tightening the architecture.
personality.motivation: I am motivated by building technically honest systems that combine research depth with real user value.
